name: Jumong
description: Jumong, also known as Dongmyeongseong, is the founding monarch of the Goguryeo kingdom, the northernmost of the Three Kingdoms of Korea. He is revered as a divine figure and a symbol of Korean national identity. According to legend, Jumong was born from an egg laid by Lady Yuhwa, who was impregnated by Haemosu, a celestial being. Faced with jealousy and threats from the Buyeo princes, Jumong fled, demonstrating his extraordinary archery skills and leadership qualities. He overcame numerous obstacles, including crossing turbulent rivers and outsmarting pursuers, eventually establishing his kingdom in 37 BCE. Jumong's reign marked the beginning of Goguryeo's expansion and influence, laying the foundation for its later dominance in the Korean peninsula. His story is one of divine birth, trials, resilience, and the establishment of a powerful nation, making him a central figure in Korean mythology and history. He is celebrated for his strategic brilliance, military prowess, and his ability to unite disparate tribes under his banner. Jumong's legacy continues to inspire Koreans, embodying themes of perseverance, courage, and national pride. His story serves as a foundational narrative for Korean identity and a testament to the enduring power of myth and legend.
subtitle: Founder of Goguryeo, Divine Archer

keyMyths.title: Birth from an Egg
keyMyths.description: Jumong was born from an egg laid by Lady Yuhwa, who was impregnated by Haemosu, a celestial being. This divine birth marks him as special and destined for greatness. The egg symbolizes his unique origin and extraordinary potential.

keyMyths.title: Escape from Buyeo
keyMyths.description: Facing threats from the Buyeo princes, Jumong fled, displaying incredible archery skills and resourcefulness. He cleverly navigated obstacles, demonstrating his leadership and determination to escape and establish his own kingdom. His escape highlights his cunning and bravery.

keyMyths.title: Founding Goguryeo
keyMyths.description: After escaping Buyeo, Jumong founded the kingdom of Goguryeo, uniting various tribes under his rule. His ability to consolidate power and establish a strong nation cemented his place as a legendary leader. The founding of Goguryeo is his greatest achievement.

sources.author: Kim Busik
sources.description: Historical record of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, including the legend of Jumong.
sources.text: Historical record of the Three Kingdoms of Korea, including the legend of Jumong.

sources.author: Iryeon
